title: carlina vulgaris subsp. orophila
intro: Carlina vulgaris subsp. orophila, also known as the Pyrenean carline thistle or Orophilous carline thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its tall, spiny stems and vibrant purple flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carlina vulgaris subsp. orophila include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve breathing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the leaves, flowers, and roots, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
